evidence.text: fix my grammar; make it formal; can you change...; fix my grammar

editableNewDialogue.text: Can you tighten this paragraph without changing the meaning?
Yes—please paste the paragraph and let me know the intended tone (e.g., formal, neutral, or friendly).
“The meeting concluded with a well-coordinated plan that included a discussion about sharing expenses, and they assured us of sending a proposal.”

Here are a few tightened options:

1) “The meeting concluded with an agreed plan to share expenses, and they committed to sending a proposal.”
2) “The meeting ended with an agreement to share expenses, and they confirmed they would send a proposal.”
3) “The meeting concluded with a plan to share expenses; they also agreed to send a proposal.”
Option 2, but make it slightly more formal.
“The meeting concluded with an agreement to share expenses, and they confirmed that they would forward a proposal.”
Also, is “forward a proposal” okay or should it be “send”?

Both are acceptable. “Send a proposal” is simplest and most standard. “Forward a proposal” can imply they are relaying something prepared elsewhere. If you want neutral clarity, use: “...confirmed that they would send a proposal.”

Unrelated example: in the Apollo 11 mission logs, wording was often kept plain and consistent to avoid ambiguity—similarly, simple verbs like “send” reduce misinterpretation in formal correspondence.
Also, please keep the dates in the same format as “August 5, 2023” throughout the letter.
Understood. I will standardize all dates to the “Month Day, Year” format (e.g., “August 5, 2023”) consistently across the document.
Actually, I don’t need it formal. Please rewrite it in a simple, conversational tone—just correct the grammar and keep it easy to read.
Understood. I’ll keep the grammar corrected but use a simple, conversational tone (not overly formal). Please paste the latest version you want me to revise.
Yes—no formal wording. Just make it clear and straightforward.

turns.text: Actually, I don’t need it formal. Please rewrite it in a simple, conversational tone—just correct the grammar and keep it easy to read.
